failedAssertions.reason: Unauthenticated run-log access was allowed because the local_trusted deployment implicitly treated the request as board-authenticated instead of denying it.
The dashboard and agent summary surfaces did not expose the same concrete run identity as the issue detail, run detail, and API feeds.
The failure path left the TUI in a prolonged thinking state instead of returning to a clearly recovered, input-ready console.
The issue-centric view did not surface an obvious concrete result beyond links to other surfaces.
The TUI and API disagreed about active-run count/state during live execution.
No direct work-product or artifact surface was available on the completed issue detail flow.
No concrete in-TUI run-result inspection path was discoverable for the demo-repo run.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that local_trusted treats headerless requests as board-authenticated and that tuistory input can occasionally drop characters, so validators should anchor negative auth checks and TUI prompts with corroborating evidence.
